perturbation.original_text: Data Recipient agrees to notify WECC within five (5) business days of the change in employment.
perturbation.changed_text: Data Recipient agrees to notify WECC within ten (10) business days of the change in employment.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'five (5) business days' to 'ten (10) business days' creates a direct conflict within the agreement regarding the timeframe for notifying WECC of employment changes. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the same obligation now has two different deadlines. It creates uncertainty as to which timeframe is enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Changes in Employment a. If at any tim e during the term of this Agreement Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient ceases to be employed by his or her then current employer, Data Recipient agrees to notify WECC within ten (10) business days of the change in employment. b. If at any time d uring the term of this Agreement Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient has a change in job responsibili lities such that Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient become s a Market Function Employee, Data Recipient agrees to notify WECC within five (5) business days of the change in employment and to ensure that Data Recipient or such an employee of Data Recipient discontinue s accessing or using any Market Sensitive Informa tion as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy .
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the notification period for employment changes from 'five (5) business days' to 'ten (10) business days'. The contradicted text, specifically section 6.b, states that notification to WECC regarding a change in employment due to a change in job responsibilities such that Data Recipient becomes a Market Function Employee must be done within 'five (5) business days'. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text says ten days in a general case when section 6.b requires five days in a specific situation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non -Public Information and supersedes all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. This Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non -Public Information and supersedes all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. This Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient. However, WECC reserves the right to unilaterally modify this agreement with 30 days written notice to Data Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence introduces an in-text contradiction regarding how the agreement can be modified. Initially, it states that the agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by BOTH WECC and Data Recipient. However, the added sentence allows WECC to unilaterally modify the agreement with 30 days written notice. This creates uncertainty about the modification process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Miscellaneous Terms a. This Agreement does not require WECC to disclose any Non -Public Information. N either this Agreement nor any disclosure of Non -Public Information grant Data Recipient any intellectual property rights or licenses to such information. Data Recipient agrees to comply with all applicable United States export laws and regulations. b. This Agreement represents the entire understanding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non -Public Information and supersedes all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. This Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient. However, WECC reserves the right to unilaterally modify this agreement with 30 days written notice to Data Recipient. Data Recipient may not delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent from WECC. Any attempt to do so is void. c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of W ECC and any owner or submitter of the Non - Public Information. This Agreement shall be fully enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non -Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement. WECC Confidentiality Agreement 4 d. If any term or provision of this Agreement is hel d by a body of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void, or unenforceable, then (1) the remaining provisions of the Agreement shall continue in full force and effect , and (2) in lieu of each such term or provision there shall be added as part of this Agr eement a clause or provision as similar in terms as possible that is legal, valid and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence that allows WECC to unilaterally modify the agreement, contradicting the original statement that the agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by both WECC and Data Recipient. The contradicted text explicitly includes this conflicting sentence, therefore a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writing that all Non -Public Information has been returned or destroyed , except as may be otherwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement shall survive termination .
perturbation.changed_text: Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writing that all Non -Public Information has been returned or destroyed. However, upon termination, Data Recipient is granted a perpetual, royalty-free license to use any Non-Public Information for internal research purposes, in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement do not survive termination .
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence creates a conflict. Initially, it requires the return or destruction of all Non-Public Information. However, the added sentence gives Data Recipient a license to use the same Non-Public Information for internal research, and it states that confidentiality obligations DO NOT survive termination in that case. This contradicts the prior requirement to either return or destroy the information, and the exception outlined previously in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Term and Termination a. This Agreement shall continue in effect until terminated. This Agreement may be terminated by WECC or Data Recipient at any time at their sole discretion. b. Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writing that all Non -Public Information has been returned or destroyed. However, upon termination, Data Recipient is granted a perpetual, royalty-free license to use any Non-Public Information for internal research purposes, in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement do not survive termination . 8. Miscellaneous Terms a. This Agreement does not require WECC to disclose any Non -Public Information. N either this Agreement nor any disclosure of Non -Public Information grant Data Recipient any intellectual property rights or licenses to such information. Data Recipient agrees to comply with all applicable United States export laws and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text require the Data Recipient to return or destroy all Non-Public Information upon termination. The changed text adds a clause granting the Data Recipient a perpetual license to use the Non-Public Information for internal research upon termination, directly conflicting with the requirement to return or destroy it. The explanation confirms this conflict, stating that the new clause 'contradicts the prior requirement to either return or destroy the information'. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be fully enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non -Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be fully enforceable only by WECC. No other entity, even those whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement, shall have the right to enforce this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change creates a direct contradiction by limiting the enforceability of the agreement. The original text states the agreement is enforceable by both WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is mishandled. The modified text explicitly restricts enforcement solely to WECC, negating the rights of other entities to enforce the agreement, even if their confidential information is compromised. This in-text contradiction would cause uncertainty as to who can take legal action if a breach occurs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Miscellaneous Terms a. This Agreement does not require WECC to disclose any Non -Public Information. N either this Agreement nor any disclosure of Non -Public Information grant Data Recipient any intellectual property rights or licenses to such information. Data Recipient agrees to comply with all applicable United States export laws and regulations. b. This Agreement represents the entire understanding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non -Public Information and supersedes all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. This Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient. However, WECC reserves the right to unilaterally modify this agreement with 30 days written notice to Data Recipient. Data Recipient may not delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent from WECC. Any attempt to do so is void. c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of W ECC and any owner or submitter of the Non - Public Information. This Agreement shall be fully enforceable only by WECC. No other entity, even those whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement, shall have the right to enforce this agreement. WECC Confidentiality Agreement 4 d. If any term or provision of this Agreement is hel d by a body of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void, or unenforceable, then (1) the remaining provisions of the Agreement shall continue in full force and effect , and (2) in lieu of each such term or provision there shall be added as part of this Agr eement a clause or provision as similar in terms as possible that is legal, valid and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly restricts the enforceability of the agreement to only WECC, stating 'No other entity...shall have the right to enforce this agreement.' This directly contradicts the original text which allowed enforcement by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with the agreement. The contradicted text reaffirms this change, stating in section c. 'This Agreement shall be fully enforceable only by WECC. No other entity, even those whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement, shall have the right to enforce this agreement.' This creates a direct in-text contradiction by negating the rights of other entities to enforce the agreement, even if their confidential information is compromised. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
